overview.short: Anikkattilammakshethram is a Hindu Temple situated in Anicadu Village, 3.5 km from Mallappally in Pathanamthitta district in the southern part of Kerala, in the peninsular India.
overview.description: Anikkattilammakshethram is a Hindu Temple situated in Anicadu Village, 3.5 km from Mallappally in Pathanamthitta district in the southern part of Kerala, in the peninsular India. In this temple, Jagat Pita and Jagat Mata (Lord Shiva and Parvati Devi) are worshiped with equal significance in one Sreekovil, which is one of the rarest of its kind in Kerala. Anthimahakalan and Ayilayekshi are symbolized in these idols, each measuring about 4 feet and installed side by side. Th Lord Shiva is in his Kiratha(Hunter)form welding a bow and an arrow in His arms and the Mother Goddess Parvati has a sword

topPlaces.name: Arohana Marthoma Church, Anicadu
topPlaces.description: Arohana Marthoma Church is a church situated in Anicadu Village, 5 km from Nedumkunnam and 4 km from Mallappally in Pathanamthitta district in the southern part of Kerala, India. It is a Malankara Mar Thoma Syrian Church under Kottayam- Kochi Dioceses.

topPlaces.name: Anicadu
topPlaces.description: Anicadu is a census village in Anicadu gram panchayat in the Mallapally taluk of the district of Pathanamthitta in Kerala, India. As of 2011, it had a population of 14,678.

topPlaces.name: Thirumalida Swayambhoo Shiva Temple
topPlaces.description: The Thirumalida Swayambhoo Shiva Temple is a Hindu Temple located in Mallappally in Pathanamthitta district in the southern part of Kerala, India. It is similar to the great Kashi Vishwanath temple and the only swayambhoo Siva temple in Kerala facing west on the banks of a river, .

topPlaces.name: Mallapally
topPlaces.description: Mallappally is a census village in the Mallappally Gram Panchayat of Mallappally taluk, Pathanamthitta district, Kerala. It falls under the Thiruvalla Revenue Division and Thiruvalla Constituency.

topPlaces.name: Keezhuvaipur
topPlaces.description: Keezhvaipur is a village in the Mallappally Taluk which is one of five Taluks that makes up the Pathanamthitta district, in Kerala State, India. It Is Part Of Thiruvalla Constituency.Keezhuvaipur lies in the bank of river Manimala and is famous for its traditions, cultures, and customs.

topPlaces.name: Vaipur
topPlaces.description: Vaipur is a village located on the banks of the Manimala River, in the Pathanamthitta District of Kerala, India. It is part of the Ranni constituency.It comes under Thiruvalla Revenue Division.

topPlaces.name: Punnavely
topPlaces.description: Punnavely is a village in Thiruvalla Constituency situated on the border of Pathanamthitta and Kottayam districts of Kerala, India. Punnavely is a typical Central Tranvancore village with rubber tree plantations, paddy fields, pepper and other spices and lot of NRIs.

topPlaces.name: Nedungadappally
topPlaces.description: Nedungadappally is a village in between Mallappally and Karukachal in Kerala, India. It is part of Thiruvalla Revenue Division, Thiruvalla constituency and Kanjirappally constituency.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Arohana Marthoma Church, Anicadu & around

items.note: Arohana Marthoma Church is a church situated in Anicadu Village, 5 km from Nedumkunnam and 4 km from Mallappally in Pathanamthitta district in the southern part of Kerala, India. It is a Malankara Mar Thoma Syrian Church under Kottayam- Kochi Dioceses.
Anicadu is a census village in Anicadu gram panchayat in the Mallapally taluk of the district of Pathanamthitta in Kerala, India. As of 2011, it had a population of 14,678.

The Thirumalida Swayambhoo Shiva Temple is a Hindu Temple located in Mallappally in Pathanamthitta district in the southern part of Kerala, India. It is similar to the great Kashi Vishwanath temple and the only swayambhoo Siva temple in Kerala facing west on the banks of a river, .
Mallappally is a census village in the Mallappally Gram Panchayat of Mallappally taluk, Pathanamthitta district, Kerala. It falls under the Thiruvalla Revenue Division and Thiruvalla Constituency.

Keezhvaipur is a village in the Mallappally Taluk which is one of five Taluks that makes up the Pathanamthitta district, in Kerala State, India. It Is Part Of Thiruvalla Constituency.Keezhuvaipur lies in the bank of river Manimala and is famous for its traditions, cultures, and customs.
Vaipur is a village located on the banks of the Manimala River, in the Pathanamthitta District of Kerala, India. It is part of the Ranni constituency.It comes under Thiruvalla Revenue Division.

Punnavely is a village in Thiruvalla Constituency situated on the border of Pathanamthitta and Kottayam districts of Kerala, India. Punnavely is a typical Central Tranvancore village with rubber tree plantations, paddy fields, pepper and other spices and lot of NRIs.
Nedungadappally is a village in between Mallappally and Karukachal in Kerala, India. It is part of Thiruvalla Revenue Division, Thiruvalla constituency and Kanjirappally constituency.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Anikkattilammakshethram?
faq.a: The best time to visit Anikkattilammakshethram is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (83 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (71 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Anikkattilammakshethram?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Anikkattilammakshethram at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Anikkattilammakshethram?
faq.a: Stays in Anikkattilammakshethram start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Anikkattilammakshethram?
faq.a: The top attractions are Arohana Marthoma Church, Anicadu, Anicadu, Thirumalida Swayambhoo Shiva Temple, Mallapally, Keezhuvaipur.
